title: Black hole jet may be stirring gas cloud containing early 'red potato' galaxy
excerpt: A black hole may be stirring a "pot" of gas containing a neighboring galaxy in the early universe, according to a new study published in Astronomy & Astrophysics that used observations from NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory. The galaxy slowly being "cooked" is named MQN01 J004131.9-493704, but astronomers have nicknamed it the "red potato" because of its appearance in images from NASA's James Webb Space Telescope.
content_html:
A black hole may be stirring a "pot" of gas containing a neighboring galaxy in the early universe, according to a new study published in Astronomy & Astrophysics that used observations from NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory. The galaxy slowly being "cooked" is named MQN01 J004131.9-493704, but astronomers have nicknamed it the "red potato" because of its appearance in images from NASA's James Webb Space Telescope.

The red potato galaxy is located about 11.7 billion light-years from Earth at an intersection where gigantic web-like structures of galaxies and gas meet. Astronomers targeted this area with Webb because they knew it contained one of the heaviest concentrations of galaxies and growing supermassive black holes yet identified in the early universe.

A missing burst of stars

Data from the European Southern Observatory's Very Large Telescope show that the red potato galaxy is surrounded by an enormous cloud, or "pot," of relatively cold gas. Astronomers expected it to have large numbers of young stars that formed after some of the gas was pulled into the galaxy. This has been seen for nearby galaxies surrounded by cold gas, but, surprisingly, not for the red potato.

"Stars are not forming like we thought they would, so we went searching for the reason why," said Weichen Wang of the University of Milan-Bicocca in Italy, who led the new study. "We found there may be a cook in this cosmic kitchen."

One important clue is that the cloud of gas surrounding the red potato galaxy is unusually turbulent compared with large gas clouds surrounding other galaxies. Such turbulence could be preventing most of the gas from falling onto the red potato galaxy to form large numbers of new stars.

A neighbor's jet as culprit

Wang and his colleagues then searched for the origin of this turmoil. Using Chandra, they discovered that a jet of particles from a black hole in a neighboring galaxy is pointed toward and may be striking the gas cloud around the red potato galaxy, possibly causing the turbulence.

"If the black hole's jet is stirring up the gas around the red potato, it could greatly slow down how quickly the galaxy can acquire new, fresh material to form stars," said co-author Sebastiano Cantalupo, also of the University of Milan-Bicocca. "With the energy from the stirring, the galaxy will starve and not be able to produce new stars at the rate expected for similar galaxies at the same cosmic epoch."

While the red potato galaxy mostly has older, cooler stars and therefore appears red in optical and infrared data, the galaxy hosting the black hole with the jet does not. Instead, this galaxy, which is located about 200,000 light-years from the red potato, is very actively forming stars, including massive, hot stars, as are most of the other nearby galaxies.

Astronomers want to learn how galaxies and black holes interact with each other—especially at this critical epoch in the universe's history—and how that affects when and how stars form.

"The red potato is leaving crumbs of information that may help us track down the answers to some really big questions," said co-author Andrea Travascio, also of the University of Milan-Bicocca. "Quite an important job for a galactic spud like this."

Other causes look less likely

The authors considered other explanations for making the gas turbulent, including outbursts from a supermassive black hole in the center of the red potato galaxy itself or energy from a burst of new stars forming. However, they consider these explanations less likely than the neighboring galaxy's jet.

This work is one of the few published studies to probe the behavior of gas surrounding passive galaxies in the early universe like the red potato, found at distances greater than about 11 billion light-years from Earth.

Data from NASA's Hubble Space Telescope was also used to support the measurements needed to reveal the galaxy's red color and low rate of star formation.

Publication details

Weichen Wang et al, A quiescent galaxy in a gas-rich cosmic web node at z ∼ 3, Astronomy & Astrophysics (2026). DOI: 10.1051/0004-6361/202659351

Who's behind this story?

Lisa Lock

content_text: A black hole may be stirring a "pot" of gas containing a neighboring galaxy in the early universe. This galaxy is located at an intersection where gigantic web-like structures of galaxies and gas meet in the early universe. A composite image shows this galaxy in X-rays from Chandra (blue), optical light data from the VLT (red), and infrared data from Webb (red, green, and blue). Evidence shows that a jet from a neighboring galaxy's black hole may be keeping the gas around the red potato turbulent, preventing it from forming large amounts of new stars as expected. Credit: X-ray: NASA/CXC/Univ. Milano-Bicocca/W. Wang et al.; Infrared: NASA/ESA/CSA/STScI; Radio: ESO/NRAO/NAOJ/ALMA; Image processing: NASA/CXC/SAO/N. Wolk & P. sentences.en: A black hole may be stirring a "pot" of gas containing a neighboring galaxy in the early universe.
sentences.zh: 黑洞可能正在搅动早期宇宙中包含邻近星系的一“锅”气体。

sentences.en: This galaxy is located at an intersection where gigantic web-like structures of galaxies and gas meet in the early universe.
sentences.zh: 这个星系位于早期宇宙中巨大的星系网状结构和气体相遇的交叉点。

sentences.en: A composite image shows this galaxy in X-rays from Chandra (blue), optical light data from the VLT (red), and infrared data from Webb (red, green, and blue).
sentences.zh: 合成图像显示了这个星系的 X 射线（蓝色）、VLT 的光学数据（红色）和韦伯的红外数据（红色、绿色和蓝色）。

sentences.en: Evidence shows that a jet from a neighboring galaxy's black hole may be keeping the gas around the red potato turbulent, preventing it from forming large amounts of new stars as expected.
sentences.zh: 有证据表明，来自邻近星系黑洞的喷流可能使红薯周围的气体保持湍流，从而阻止它按预期形成大量新恒星。

sentences.en: Credit: X-ray: NASA/CXC/Univ.
sentences.zh: 图片来源：X 射线：NASA/CXC/Univ。

sentences.zh: 米兰-比可卡/W.

blocks.html_en: A black hole may be stirring a "pot" of gas containing a neighboring galaxy in the early universe, according to a new study published in Astronomy & Astrophysics that used observations from NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory. The galaxy slowly being "cooked" is named MQN01 J004131.9-493704, but astronomers have nicknamed it the "red potato" because of its appearance in images from NASA's James Webb Space Telescope.

sentences.en: A black hole may be stirring a "pot" of gas containing a neighboring galaxy in the early universe, according to a new study published in Astronomy & Astrophysics that used observations from NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory.
sentences.zh: 根据美国宇航局钱德拉 X 射线天文台的观测结果，发表在《天文学与天体物理学》上的一项新研究表明，黑洞可能正在搅动早期宇宙中包含邻近星系的一“锅”气体。

sentences.en: The galaxy slowly being "cooked" is named MQN01 J004131.9-493704, but astronomers have nicknamed it the "red potato" because of its appearance in images from NASA's James Webb Space Telescope.
sentences.zh: 这个慢慢被“煮熟”的星系被命名为MQN01 J004131.9-493704，但天文学家们给它起了个绰号“红土豆”，因为它出现在美国宇航局詹姆斯·韦伯太空望远镜的图像中。

blocks.html_en: The red potato galaxy is located about 11.7 billion light-years from Earth at an intersection where gigantic web-like structures of galaxies and gas meet. Astronomers targeted this area with Webb because they knew it contained one of the heaviest concentrations of galaxies and growing supermassive black holes yet identified in the early universe.

sentences.en: The red potato galaxy is located about 11.7 billion light-years from Earth at an intersection where gigantic web-like structures of galaxies and gas meet.
sentences.zh: 红薯星系距离地球约 117 亿光年，位于星系和气体的巨大网状结构交汇处。

sentences.en: Astronomers targeted this area with Webb because they knew it contained one of the heaviest concentrations of galaxies and growing supermassive black holes yet identified in the early universe.
sentences.zh: 天文学家将韦伯瞄准了这个区域，因为他们知道这里是早期宇宙中迄今为止发现的星系和不断增长的超大质量黑洞最密集的区域之一。

blocks.html_en: A missing burst of stars

sentences.en: A missing burst of stars
sentences.zh: 缺失的星星爆发

blocks.html_en: Data from the European Southern Observatory's Very Large Telescope show that the red potato galaxy is surrounded by an enormous cloud, or "pot," of relatively cold gas. Astronomers expected it to have large numbers of young stars that formed after some of the gas was pulled into the galaxy. This has been seen for nearby galaxies surrounded by cold gas, but, surprisingly, not for the red potato.

sentences.en: Data from the European Southern Observatory's Very Large Telescope show that the red potato galaxy is surrounded by an enormous cloud, or "pot," of relatively cold gas.
sentences.zh: 来自欧洲南方天文台甚大望远镜的数据显示，红马铃薯星系被一个巨大的云团或相对较冷的气体“锅”包围。

sentences.en: Astronomers expected it to have large numbers of young stars that formed after some of the gas was pulled into the galaxy.
sentences.zh: 天文学家预计它会拥有大量年轻恒星，这些恒星是在一些气体被吸入星系后形成的。

sentences.en: This has been seen for nearby galaxies surrounded by cold gas, but, surprisingly, not for the red potato.
sentences.zh: 在被冷气体包围的附近星系中已经看到了这种情况，但令人惊讶的是，红薯却没有。

blocks.html_en: "Stars are not forming like we thought they would, so we went searching for the reason why," said Weichen Wang of the University of Milan-Bicocca in Italy, who led the new study. "We found there may be a cook in this cosmic kitchen."

sentences.en: "Stars are not forming like we thought they would, so we went searching for the reason why," said Weichen Wang of the University of Milan-Bicocca in Italy, who led the new study.
sentences.zh: 领导这项新研究的意大利米兰比可卡大学的 Weichen Wang 表示：“恒星的形成并不像我们想象的那样，因此我们开始寻找原因。”

sentences.en: "We found there may be a cook in this cosmic kitchen."
sentences.zh: “我们发现这个宇宙厨房里可能有一个厨师。”

blocks.html_en: One important clue is that the cloud of gas surrounding the red potato galaxy is unusually turbulent compared with large gas clouds surrounding other galaxies. Such turbulence could be preventing most of the gas from falling onto the red potato galaxy to form large numbers of new stars.

sentences.en: One important clue is that the cloud of gas surrounding the red potato galaxy is unusually turbulent compared with large gas clouds surrounding other galaxies.
sentences.zh: 一个重要的线索是，与其他星系周围的大型气体云相比，红薯星系周围的气体云异常湍急。

sentences.en: Such turbulence could be preventing most of the gas from falling onto the red potato galaxy to form large numbers of new stars.
sentences.zh: 这种湍流可能会阻止大部分气体落到红薯星系上形成大量新恒星。

blocks.html_en: A neighbor's jet as culprit

sentences.en: A neighbor's jet as culprit
sentences.zh: 邻居的飞机是罪魁祸首

blocks.html_en: Wang and his colleagues then searched for the origin of this turmoil. Using Chandra, they discovered that a jet of particles from a black hole in a neighboring galaxy is pointed toward and may be striking the gas cloud around the red potato galaxy, possibly causing the turbulence.

sentences.en: Wang and his colleagues then searched for the origin of this turmoil.
sentences.zh: 王和他的同事随后寻找这场骚乱的根源。

sentences.en: Using Chandra, they discovered that a jet of particles from a black hole in a neighboring galaxy is pointed toward and may be striking the gas cloud around the red potato galaxy, possibly causing the turbulence.
sentences.zh: 利用钱德拉，他们发现来自邻近星系黑洞的一股粒子流指向并可能撞击红薯星系周围的气体云，从而可能引起湍流。

blocks.html_en: "If the black hole's jet is stirring up the gas around the red potato, it could greatly slow down how quickly the galaxy can acquire new, fresh material to form stars," said co-author Sebastiano Cantalupo, also of the University of Milan-Bicocca. "With the energy from the stirring, the galaxy will starve and not be able to produce new stars at the rate expected for similar galaxies at the same cosmic epoch."

sentences.en: "If the black hole's jet is stirring up the gas around the red potato, it could greatly slow down how quickly the galaxy can acquire new, fresh material to form stars," said co-author Sebastiano Cantalupo, also of the University of Milan-Bicocca.
sentences.zh: 米兰比可卡大学的合著者塞巴斯蒂亚诺·坎塔卢波说：“如果黑洞的喷流搅动了红薯周围的气体，它可能会大大减慢星系获得新的、新鲜的物质形成恒星的速度。”

sentences.en: "With the energy from the stirring, the galaxy will starve and not be able to produce new stars at the rate expected for similar galaxies at the same cosmic epoch."
sentences.zh: “由于搅拌产生的能量，星系将陷入饥饿，并且无法以同一宇宙时期类似星系的预期速度产生新恒星。”

blocks.html_en: While the red potato galaxy mostly has older, cooler stars and therefore appears red in optical and infrared data, the galaxy hosting the black hole with the jet does not. Instead, this galaxy, which is located about 200,000 light-years from the red potato, is very actively forming stars, including massive, hot stars, as are most of the other nearby galaxies.

sentences.en: While the red potato galaxy mostly has older, cooler stars and therefore appears red in optical and infrared data, the galaxy hosting the black hole with the jet does not.
sentences.zh: 虽然红薯星系大多拥有较老、较冷的恒星，因此在光学和红外数据中呈现红色，但拥有喷流的黑洞的星系却不然。

sentences.en: Instead, this galaxy, which is located about 200,000 light-years from the red potato, is very actively forming stars, including massive, hot stars, as are most of the other nearby galaxies.
sentences.zh: 相反，这个距离红薯约20万光年的星系正在非常活跃地形成恒星，包括大质量的炽热恒星，就像大多数其他附近的星系一样。

blocks.html_en: Astronomers want to learn how galaxies and black holes interact with each other—especially at this critical epoch in the universe's history—and how that affects when and how stars form.

sentences.en: Astronomers want to learn how galaxies and black holes interact with each other—especially at this critical epoch in the universe's history—and how that affects when and how stars form.
sentences.zh: 天文学家希望了解星系和黑洞如何相互作用——尤其是在宇宙历史的这个关键时期——以及它如何影响恒星形成的时间和方式。

blocks.html_en: "The red potato is leaving crumbs of information that may help us track down the answers to some really big questions," said co-author Andrea Travascio, also of the University of Milan-Bicocca. "Quite an important job for a galactic spud like this."

sentences.en: "The red potato is leaving crumbs of information that may help us track down the answers to some really big questions," said co-author Andrea Travascio, also of the University of Milan-Bicocca.
sentences.zh: “红薯留下的信息碎片可能会帮助我们找到一些真正重大问题的答案，”共同作者、米兰比可卡大学的安德里亚·特拉瓦西奥（Andrea Travascio）说。

sentences.en: "Quite an important job for a galactic spud like this."
sentences.zh: “对于像这样的银河马铃薯来说，这是一项相当重要的工作。”

blocks.html_en: Other causes look less likely

sentences.en: Other causes look less likely
sentences.zh: 其他原因看起来不太可能

blocks.html_en: The authors considered other explanations for making the gas turbulent, including outbursts from a supermassive black hole in the center of the red potato galaxy itself or energy from a burst of new stars forming. However, they consider these explanations less likely than the neighboring galaxy's jet.

sentences.en: The authors considered other explanations for making the gas turbulent, including outbursts from a supermassive black hole in the center of the red potato galaxy itself or energy from a burst of new stars forming.
sentences.zh: 作者考虑了导致气体湍流的其他解释，包括红薯星系本身中心的超大质量黑洞的爆发或新恒星形成爆发的能量。

sentences.en: However, they consider these explanations less likely than the neighboring galaxy's jet.
sentences.zh: 然而，他们认为这些解释的可能性不如邻近星系的喷流。

blocks.html_en: This work is one of the few published studies to probe the behavior of gas surrounding passive galaxies in the early universe like the red potato, found at distances greater than about 11 billion light-years from Earth.

sentences.en: This work is one of the few published studies to probe the behavior of gas surrounding passive galaxies in the early universe like the red potato, found at distances greater than about 11 billion light-years from Earth.
sentences.zh: 这项工作是为数不多的已发表的研究之一，旨在探索早期宇宙中被动星系（如红薯）周围气体的行为，这些星系距离地球约 110 亿光年。

blocks.html_en: Data from NASA's Hubble Space Telescope was also used to support the measurements needed to reveal the galaxy's red color and low rate of star formation.

sentences.en: Data from NASA's Hubble Space Telescope was also used to support the measurements needed to reveal the galaxy's red color and low rate of star formation.
sentences.zh: 美国宇航局哈勃太空望远镜的数据也被用来支持揭示星系的红色和低恒星形成率所需的测量。

content_html_zh:
根据美国宇航局钱德拉 X 射线天文台的观测结果，发表在《天文学与天体物理学》上的一项新研究表明，黑洞可能正在搅动早期宇宙中包含邻近星系的一“锅”气体。 这个慢慢被“煮熟”的星系被命名为MQN01 J004131.9-493704，但天文学家们给它起了个绰号“红土豆”，因为它出现在美国宇航局詹姆斯·韦伯太空望远镜的图像中。

红薯星系距离地球约 117 亿光年，位于星系和气体的巨大网状结构交汇处。 天文学家将韦伯瞄准了这个区域，因为他们知道这里是早期宇宙中迄今为止发现的星系和不断增长的超大质量黑洞最密集的区域之一。

缺失的星星爆发

来自欧洲南方天文台甚大望远镜的数据显示，红马铃薯星系被一个巨大的云团或相对较冷的气体“锅”包围。 天文学家预计它会拥有大量年轻恒星，这些恒星是在一些气体被吸入星系后形成的。 在被冷气体包围的附近星系中已经看到了这种情况，但令人惊讶的是，红薯却没有。

领导这项新研究的意大利米兰比可卡大学的 Weichen Wang 表示：“恒星的形成并不像我们想象的那样，因此我们开始寻找原因。” “我们发现这个宇宙厨房里可能有一个厨师。”

一个重要的线索是，与其他星系周围的大型气体云相比，红薯星系周围的气体云异常湍急。 这种湍流可能会阻止大部分气体落到红薯星系上形成大量新恒星。

邻居的飞机是罪魁祸首

王和他的同事随后寻找这场骚乱的根源。 利用钱德拉，他们发现来自邻近星系黑洞的一股粒子流指向并可能撞击红薯星系周围的气体云，从而可能引起湍流。

米兰比可卡大学的合著者塞巴斯蒂亚诺·坎塔卢波说：“如果黑洞的喷流搅动了红薯周围的气体，它可能会大大减慢星系获得新的、新鲜的物质形成恒星的速度。” “由于搅拌产生的能量，星系将陷入饥饿，并且无法以同一宇宙时期类似星系的预期速度产生新恒星。”

虽然红薯星系大多拥有较老、较冷的恒星，因此在光学和红外数据中呈现红色，但拥有喷流的黑洞的星系却不然。 相反，这个距离红薯约20万光年的星系正在非常活跃地形成恒星，包括大质量的炽热恒星，就像大多数其他附近的星系一样。

天文学家希望了解星系和黑洞如何相互作用——尤其是在宇宙历史的这个关键时期——以及它如何影响恒星形成的时间和方式。

“红薯留下的信息碎片可能会帮助我们找到一些真正重大问题的答案，”共同作者、米兰比可卡大学的安德里亚·特拉瓦西奥（Andrea Travascio）说。 “对于像这样的银河马铃薯来说，这是一项相当重要的工作。”

其他原因看起来不太可能

作者考虑了导致气体湍流的其他解释，包括红薯星系本身中心的超大质量黑洞的爆发或新恒星形成爆发的能量。 然而，他们认为这些解释的可能性不如邻近星系的喷流。

这项工作是为数不多的已发表的研究之一，旨在探索早期宇宙中被动星系（如红薯）周围气体的行为，这些星系距离地球约 110 亿光年。

美国宇航局哈勃太空望远镜的数据也被用来支持揭示星系的红色和低恒星形成率所需的测量。

出版详情

Weichen Wang 等人，z ∼ 3 处富含气体的宇宙网节点中的静止星系，天文学与天体物理学（2026）。 DOI：10.1051/0004-6361/202659351

谁是这个故事的幕后黑手？

丽莎·洛克

艺术史学士，物质文化硕士。 前博物馆编辑、护理人员和移植协调员。 自 2021 年起为 Science X 进行编辑。 完整档案 →

罗伯特·伊根

数学生物学学士学位，创意写作硕士学位。 游历广泛，对科学和语言有独特的观点。 完整档案 →
